children.name: Briareum asbestinum
children.description: {{Speciesbox | name = Corky sea finger | image = Dedos de muerto.jpg | image_caption = In Pinar del Río Province, Cuba | taxon = Briareum asbestinum | authority = (Pallas, 1766) | synonyms = * Alcyonium asbestinum Pallas, 1766 * Ammothea polyanthes Duchassaing & Michelotti, 1860 * Asbestia asbestinum (Pallas, 1766) * Briarea asbestina (Pallas, 1766) * Briareum gorgonoideum Blainville, 1830 * Briareum marquesarum (Kükenthal, 1916) * Briareum polyanthes (Duchassaing & Michelotti, 1860) * Erythropodium marquesarum Kükenthal, 1916 * Erythropodium polyanthes (Duchassaing & Michelotti, 1860) * Gorgonia briareus Ellis & Solander, 1786 * Lobularia asbestina (Pallas, 1766) * Nephthya polyanthus (Duchassaing & Michelotti, 1860) * Solenopodium marquesarum (Kükenthal, 1916) * Solenopodium polyanthes (Duchassaing & Michelotti, 1860) * Titanideum hartmeyeri Kükenthal, 1908 * Vioa asbestina (Pallas, 1766) }} thumb|right|240px|Small colony at [[Molasses Reef in 2023]] Briareum asbestinum, commonly known as the corky sea finger, is a species of a soft coral in the family Briareidae.
